title: Finite Gram-Schmidt Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Gram-Schmidt bad projection coefficient QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact Gram-Schmidt replay computes r12 = q1 dot a2 = 3/5 while a malformed row claims 4/5, isolating a compact equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional orthogonalization rows only when they add distinct projection, normalization, QR-product, least-squares, residual, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general Gram-Schmidt/QR correctness, rank-deficient variants, conditioning, and floating-point stability in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational input vectors, normalized first vector, projection coefficient, residual vector, normalized second vector, Q matrix, R matrix, and product matrix as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false projection coefficient row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes r12 = 3/5.
Expose the pack through matrix computation, QR/factorization, inner-product/projection, and exact-vs-floating learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep general Gram-Schmidt/QR correctness, rank-deficient variants, conditioning, and floating-point stability marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
